note: Golden cases transcribed from US Navy Diving Manual Rev 7 Change A (2018), Tables 9-7 (page 9-63) and 9-9 (pages 9-65..9-79), rendered from https://www.divetable.info/workshop/USN_Rev7_Tables.pdf and read cell-by-cell. These assert real Navy cell values, not placeholder mechanics. Rounding: depth up to next tabulated depth, bottom time up to next tabulated time. 9-9 total_stop_min is the SUM of the AIR stop-column minutes (not the manual's Total Ascent Time). See engine/tables/SOURCES.md for verification status.

no_deco_cases.description: 60 fsw / 25 min: exact depth, time rounds up to 28 (E) within NDL 63
Depth round-up (55 -> 60); 20 min rounds up to 22 (D column) within NDL 63... note 55 is tabulated so no round-up needed here (D at 55/25)
100 fsw / 12 min rounds up to 15 (D column), NDL 25
40 fsw / 30 min: exact D-column cell (max_time 36 -> D), NDL 163
190 fsw / 4 min rounds up to 5 (D column), deepest air row, NDL 5

deco_cases.description: 60 fsw / 70 min exceeds 63-min NDL -> Table 9-9 (AIR: 7 min at 20 fsw, group L)
100 fsw / 30 min exceeds 25-min NDL -> Table 9-9 (AIR: 3 min at 20 fsw, group J)
150 fsw / 50 min -> exceptional-exposure schedule (AIR stops 50/40/30/20 fsw = 4/14/28/170 = 216 min, group Z)
170 fsw / 25 min -> Table 9-9 (AIR stops 50/40/30/20 fsw = 1/7/7/41 = 56 min, group O, non-exceptional)
190 fsw / 35 min -> exceptional-exposure schedule (AIR stops 70/60/50/40/30/20 fsw = 4/5/6/8/26/165 = 214 min, group Z)
